tests_checks_run: Static inspection only; no notebook execution, manuscript build, code tests, or simulations were run.
result: Completed read-only manuscript Section II mapping and wrote the requested report pair.
risks: The ordered-link sign convention is the main regression risk.
The manuscript indexes h_{i,j} as node i transmitting to UE j, while package code stores links as (receiver_node_id, transmitter_node_id).
The code and manuscript are consistent only when this mapping is applied explicitly.
The manuscript uses meter/time-domain notation, while package modules use kilometer/range-equivalent clock variables.
recommended_next_action: Require every notebook/package measurement row to carry explicit receiver_node_id, transmitter_node_id, link_type, and range-domain units before comparing against V24 manuscript equations.
scope_boundary_encountered: No manuscript, notebook, package code, test, figure, or existing result-output files were edited or executed.

manuscript_objects.expected_code_counterpart: len(config.links) in V24ScenarioConfig.validate; link builders in jcls_sim/configs.py
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Directed SL count implies all ordered UE-to-UE links except self-links.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: Each satellite broadcasts downlink reference signals to each UE.
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Code tuple is receiver first and transmitter second.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: UEs form a terrestrial ad hoc mesh; directed count is N_u^2 - N_u.
manuscript_objects.expected_code_counterpart: directed_sidelink_links(num_users) returns all (receiver, transmitter) UE pairs with receiver not equal transmitter.
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Matches count when all directed links are used; minimal_sidelink_links is a reduced diagnostic graph.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: RV{z} in R^{N_z}; one measurement per signal; measurement-agnostic and may contain heterogeneous measurements.
manuscript_objects.expected_code_counterpart: Observed arrays computed as z = h_theta + noise in package runners, in km.
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Manuscript writes meters; package diagnostics use kilometers.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: Stacked parameterized range model V{h}(theta) in R^{N_z}.
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Code row order is the supplied links order; manuscript stack order is by receiving UE j.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: Measurement from node i to UE j in meters: ||p_i-p_j|| + c(delta_i-delta_j), with delta_{Nu+1}=0.
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Equivalent if manuscript (i,j) maps to code (receiver=j, transmitter=i) and clock variables are range-equivalent km.
Code order matches positions, UE clocks, and non-reference satellite clocks.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: First satellite is the reference clock; delta_{Nu+1} is fixed at zero.
manuscript_objects.expected_code_counterpart: reference_satellite_node_id(num_users) = Nu + 1; full_clock_dict_km sets this node to 0.0.

manuscript_objects.audit_note: Manuscript clock offsets are time-domain in c delta; code stores range-equivalent offsets.
Matches exclusion of the first satellite.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: Cov(z) = E[n n^T]; for LOS/Rician Gaussian approximation, R_z = diag(sigma^2).
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Matches diagonal Gaussian/Rician case in range-domain km units.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: Positive range-domain standard deviations, with entries selected according to link conditions.
manuscript_objects.expected_code_counterpart: range_std_devs_km validated per link in V24ScenarioConfig.validate
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Code supports per-link values but does not by itself encode LOS/NLOS likelihood class.

manuscript_objects.manuscript_definition: Range measurements are in meters; scalar pseudorange z = c tau_hat; delay-domain densities map to range-domain through c.
manuscript_objects.expected_code_counterpart: Package variables use _km; clock offsets are range-equivalent km.
manuscript_objects.audit_note: Clock seconds/nanoseconds must be converted through speed of light before comparing to theta/code clocks.

expected_code_counterparts.required_invariant: Reference satellite node id is exactly Nu+1.
No reference satellite clock column is present.
Link order defines row order and must be documented.
Equivalent to manuscript satellite-to-UE DL only if transmitter is the satellite.
Full manuscript graph uses all ordered UE pairs except self-links.
Equivalent to manuscript line 606 under transmitter-to-receiver interpretation.
Only the diagonal Gaussian/Rician covariance model is represented by this helper.

ambiguities.detail: Manuscript line 606 says measurement from node i to UE j and uses c(delta_i-delta_j). Code functions use (receiver_node_id, transmitter_node_id) and compute transmitter - receiver.
ambiguities.impact: Consistent only if manuscript h_{i,j} maps to code link (j, i).
This can be consistent with code if i is receiver and j is transmitter, but it differs from the manuscript's transmitter-to-receiver indexing prose.

ambiguities.detail: Section II allows z to contain heterogeneous measurements, while inspected package helpers are range-domain TOA/Gaussian-centric.
ambiguities.impact: Regression artifacts should not imply the package currently covers every heterogeneous likelihood allowed by manuscript prose.

ambiguities.detail: Manuscript stacks h_j by receiving UE; code evaluates rows in supplied link order.
Manuscript equations use meters and time-domain clock offsets through c delta; package code uses km and range-equivalent clock offsets.
ambiguities.impact: Synchronization metrics and FIM/Jacobian scaling can be wrong if conversion is skipped.

mismatches.evidence: Manuscript line 606 uses transmitter-to-receiver prose; code links are receiver/transmitter tuples.
mismatches.consequence: Reversing receiver and transmitter flips the clock-bias sign while leaving Euclidean range unchanged.

mismatches.evidence: Manuscript writes c(delta_i-delta_j); code clock variables are _km and Jacobian clock derivatives are +/-1.
mismatches.consequence: Directly comparing clock values without speed-of-light and km/m conversion can corrupt synchronization metrics or FIM scaling.

mismatches.risk: Full directed SL graph versus reduced diagnostics
mismatches.evidence: Manuscript count is N_u^2-N_u; code also has minimal_sidelink_links.
mismatches.consequence: Using minimal SL diagnostics as manuscript-equivalent would understate measurement count and observability.

mismatches.evidence: Section II distinguishes LOS/Rician Gaussian covariance from NLOS density convolution.
mismatches.consequence: CRLB/FIM diagnostics using diag(sigma^2) should be labeled Gaussian/Rician or approximate.

mismatches.evidence: Manuscript stacks by UE; code stacks according to supplied links.
mismatches.consequence: Notebook/package regression can falsely pass or fail if rows are compared without link metadata.

bottom_line: The inspected package modules have the right V24 gauge, parameter dimension, theta layout, diagonal range covariance, and receiver/transmitter range model needed to represent Section II. The main care points are sign-convention naming and units: code links are (receiver, transmitter) and code clocks are range-equivalent kilometers, whereas the manuscript describes a transmission from node i to UE j and writes clock offsets through c delta.
